title: chenopodium album subsp. ambrosioides
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. ambrosioides, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a plant species in the goosefoot family (Chenopodiaceae) that is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium album subsp. ambrosioides include relief from digestive issues, such as gas and bloating, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever reduction and skin conditions. Chenopodium album subsp. ambrosioides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or added to food as a spice, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
